commit.message:
Fix AI time awareness and AEIOU voice loop bugs

- System prompt: inject current PST time (currentTimePST) alongside the
  date so AI uses the actual hour when picking preferred_time defaults
  instead of always defaulting to 08:00/18:00 regardless of context

- AEIOU voice mode: fix stale closure bug causing voice to stop after
  the 3rd question. sendToAi/submitAeiou/startConversation were capturing
  stale startListening/stopListening references because those closures are
  defined before useDeepgramSTT runs. Fix: use startListeningRef and
  stopListeningRef that are kept in sync via useEffect.

- AEIOU [AEIOU_COMPLETE] with empty JSON: was returning early without
  resetting isProcessingRef, permanently locking voice input. Now resets
  the ref and resumes listening in that edge case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: card ambient breathing + section aurora backgrounds

Cards now breathe at rest with desynchronized inner glow drift:
- FeaturesSection (12 cards), GoalCards (3), UseCaseCards (3),
  SmartOptimization (3), AdditionalFeatures (2)
- ::before pseudo-element with blurred radial gradient
- 8s drift cycle with prime-offset delays per nth-child
- Complements existing hover effects (glow shows at rest, hover takes over)

Section-level aurora background on Features, Goals, SmartOptimization:
- Slow 25s gradient drift behind section content
- Extremely subtle (0.03-0.04 opacity), blur(40px)

All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion.

commit.message:
Add prefix-first playground with 19 prefix matrices

New feature: the Prefixes and Suffixes category now launches a dedicated
PrefixPlayground where the prefix is the fixed anchor and students click
base word chips to form words.

- src/data/prefixMatrices.ts: 19 matrices across 4 categories
  (Negation: un/dis/non/in/im/il/ir/mis, Time: re/pre/fore,
   Location: sub/inter/trans/super/mid, Amount: semi/under/over)
  Each matrix has 4–10 base chips, optional suffix chips, and a full
  word key with kid-friendly definitions and example sentences.

- src/components/student/PrefixPlayground.tsx: new click-based playground.
  Prefix is displayed as fixed; student taps a base chip to build a word,
  optionally adds a suffix chip, then goes through the same
  Judge → Meaning Check → Sentence → Result flow as StudentPlayground.

- src/components/student/StudentSelect.tsx: replaced old PrefixSuffixPicker
  with a two-level picker: category (Negation / Time / Location / Amount)
  → specific prefix → PrefixPlayground.

- src/App.tsx: added 'prefix-play' screen type and routing.

commit.message:
Fix 3 appeal letter bugs: visit date, owner name, circular citations

BUG 1: visit_date showed "a recent date" because frontend service_date
was null. Now fetches service_date from pciq_analyses DB as fallback,
and formats to human-readable (e.g., "January 27, 2026").

BUG 2: owner_name showed "Neos Pet Parent" because user_metadata
full_name was null. Now uses fallback chain: user_metadata > profiles
table > "[Your Name]". Never falls back to pet name.

BUG 3: policy citations were circular — every item cited "Covered -
diagnostic" as both category AND policy language. Now fetches original
line_items from DB to find real sourceQuote values. Detects circular
citations (starting with "Covered" or matching coverage_reason) and
replaces with real policy quote from original analysis, or generic
fallback if no real quote exists.

commit.message:
fix: accept st-docker-test entry point in validate-local preflight (#218)

* fix: accept st-docker-test entry point in validate-local preflight

st-validate-local now checks for st-docker-test (installed entry point)
in addition to docker-test (legacy bash wrapper) when verifying that
docker-test is available.

_find_validator also prefers st-{name} entry points over bare names,
ensuring validators are found when standard-tooling is pip-installed
and scripts/bin/ is not on PATH.

Co-Authored-By: wphillipmoore-claude <255925739+wphillipmoore-claude@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add PYTHONPATH fallback to bash wrappers for non-installed environments

CI (standards-compliance action) clones standard-tooling and puts
scripts/bin/ on PATH without pip-installing the package, so the
st-* entry points are not available. The wrappers now try the entry
point first, then fall back to running the Python module directly
with PYTHONPATH pointing to the source tree.

Ref #217

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: temporarily skip standards-compliance to bootstrap wrapper fix

The standards-compliance action checks out standard-tooling from
develop, which now has broken exec-only wrappers from #216. This
PR fixes the wrappers with a PYTHONPATH fallback, but CI cannot
pass until the fix lands on develop.

Ref #217

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: wphillipmoore-claude <255925739+wphillipmoore-claude@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sports team letter-matching rule: use distinctive name, not city

The letter is determined by the team's distinctive name, not the city prefix.
- Real mascot → use it: Chicago Bears = B, Toronto Maple Leafs = M
- Generic suffix (City, United) → use the city: Manchester City = M
- Strip title/corporate prefixes (FC, Real, AC, RB, Red Bull)
- Shared prefixes (Dynamo, Maccabi, CSKA) → use the city
- Rejection explanations teach the rule to players

Applied to both validation and appeal prompts.

commit.message:
Rewrite mobile touch drag — immediate response, no long-press delay

Root cause: touchstart was { passive: true }, so the browser committed
to scrolling before our drag code could intervene. The 300ms long-press
delay made it worse — by the time drag started, scroll was already active.

New approach:
- touchstart is { passive: false } — when touching a node/container,
  call preventDefault() immediately to block scroll from the first frame
- No delay: drag begins as soon as finger moves 8px (drag threshold)
- Touch on empty canvas still scrolls normally (no preventDefault)
- state.dragging is set up directly from node/group IDs, not via
  synthetic mousedown (avoids stale DOM element issues after render)
- mousemove/mouseup synthetic events drive app.js drag logic reliably
- Node follows finger continuously until finger lifts

commit.message:
Fix latestHour < earliestHour causing zero scheduling window, clear stale slots

Two bugs found from debug logs:
1. latestHour=3 (3AM) with earliestHour=9 gave dayEnd=180 < dayStart=540.
   The fallback reset re-applied the same broken values. Now handles
   wrap-past-midnight (3AM→capped at 24:00) and falls back to 9-23 hardcoded.
2. Current-week DELETE only removed today+future unlocked slots, so stale
   slots from previous buggy generations on past days (e.g. Sunday) survived.
   Now deletes all unlocked slots for the entire week range.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Decouple provider config key from provider type

Allow arbitrary config keys in auth.providers by introducing an optional
`provider` field that declares the provider type. This enables multiple
instances of the same provider type (e.g. two Skrift hubs) and custom
keys like `corporate_sso`. When `provider` is absent, the key is used
as the type for full backward compatibility.
